title: When Status changes to DONE, Remove "Assignee" from People
excerpt:
I love that I can auto-assign a person aka assignee, to a task when I create it the item. 
I’d like to be able to do the same in reverse: when I change a Status to Done, the automation clears the Person column, to empty. …

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml:
I love that I can auto-assign a person aka assignee, to a task when I create it the item.

I’d like to be able to do the same in reverse: when I change a Status to Done, the automation clears the Person column, to empty.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great thought - I’d love to pass this onto our product team for review - any other ideas that might be of similar nature?

Look forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
YES @lauralev!!!

We’d like to automate who IS assigned next, based on status.

Example Use Case:

1 - We have an internal status column (with a lot more detail than our customer facing statuses), one of these is Ready for Testing.
2 - When this status is achieved in internal status it triggers a testing status change (maybe Internal Testing or similar).
3 - When this occurs, we’d like to make the Currently Assigned person field on the item reflect someone we’ve identified as a tester or team of testers. without having to teach people to remember to make that update when they remember to make the status update while they remember to record their testing results.

This would help us narrow down notifications to those assigned next which is critical to making Monday’s notifications useful (in some areas). Too many notifications is worse than none at all (many times).

We have lots of similar use cases (like content review and other workflow based tasks).

Thanks for logging this @cwiassoc!

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Get @straister

Can you send us a screenshot of the board that you’re using the manage the use case above so we can take a look at how you have it set up so far?

Thanks!

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
We don’t have a board set up this way yet, but the example concept would be tester columns, reviewer columns etc (which can be set up at the onset of a project or pulse). The “Next Up” or “Currently Assigned to” column would be updated (based on the combination of Status and People column.

Please let me know if that clears this up any?

Thanks!
Shell

posts.author: Nichol (Nichol Goines)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! I would love to be able to change assignee based on status. I have to do everything manually now. For example, I use my boards for product management (marketing). So I will have one item and a column for product testing, purchasing, marketing and so on. I don’t want to have to have multiple people columns too. It would be nice to assign the first person and process in the project, then when he changes the testing status to done, it assigns me to project and I can update the marketing status. Then when I mark as done, it assigns our purchasing dept to take it from there.
Currently, I get notifications for EVERY thing so I don’t miss what I am supposed to do…
Basically, our New products team is using Monday.com like a PLM or PIM system…the board is huge and linked to so many things I have to have my own board just so I can digest the information for my team.

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
yes!! @lauralev!

We use the attached automation and steps 1. and 2. of what @straister mentioned. However, the newly Assigned person does not know what column under their To-Do is necessarily associated with them because we have this new status that may have a different due date.

Screen Shot 2019-10-08 at 11.13.18 PM.png2382×1316 125 KB

posts.author: Danish (Klaus)
posts.cookedHtml:
How come is not possible to select “none” (or similar) as “someone” from the recipe that @abiciea posted above?

That would allow us to remove an assignee from a pulse, whenever the status changes to “something”

Did a miss a recipe, or is it still not possible to have an automation remove an assignee based on status change?

Thanks.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
As far as I know it it not possible. But this is a really important automation to keep your task lists clean. There’s some other questions about this same issue in the community

When Status changes to DONE, Remove due date / date Feature requests
Great suggestion, I have the same issue. Items that are Done but have a date in the future, still show in the google calendar as To be done. This is confusing. My suggestion for a new automation would be: When status X change to X, clear column X from this item. This would be applicable to the Date and People column as wel. Also see When Status changes to DONE, Remove "Assignee" from People and Unassign when issue is set to done

posts.author: AnyaP (Anya P)
posts.cookedHtml:
Like others on this thread, I’d like to be able to remove people (slash switch people, slash add specific people and not just from ‘item supported fields’) people based on item status.

I think I figured out one way around this. If you move a “Done” item to a different board (not group), then you can simply leave the “People” column empty upon transfer (in my image, “TECH”).

image737×685 36 KB

This is potentially inconvenient in other ways (navigating between different boards), but I figure with Done items, it could work out…

It would be nice to have the same transfer columns option available for moving between groups, not just boards.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
All,

There is a little free app that will do this for you. It presents itself in the automations, so you can build anything you want, like:

image1510×434 23.1 KB

The app can be installed from monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

posts.author: bade (Mikada)
posts.cookedHtml:
Introducing Auto Assign App Automate Your Task Assignments for Maximum Productivity!

Imagine having the power to add your board,

• Automation to Assign Person who makes changes to the Status

• Automation to Add Person who makes changes to the Status as assignee

• Automation to Remove Person who made change the status from the people column

But Auto Assign doesn’t stop there. Our developers are open to your feedback to develop more auto-assign automation!
